title: S3 Presigning and CloudFront Signed Access in TypeScript · Type Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs s3 presigning and cloudfront signed access in typescript from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for typed signed-delivery contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose signed URL or signed cookie by object count, path scope, player behavior, client constraints, and leakage surface.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose canned or custom CloudFront policy and constrain expiry, optional start time, resource wildcard, and IP range without accidental overbreadth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript S3 Presigned Download · changed case · Review S3 Presigned Download
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7370 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound SDK v3 signed access by exact time, resource, key, and bearer handling; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.

Which action demonstrates S3 Presigned Download without dropping earlier contracts?
